name: Virgen del Socorro Trail
name_original: Sagrado Corazón de Jesús — circular trailrunning desde Sacedón
description: Jog the moderate Virgen del Socorro Trail, a 4.5-mile route with 819 feet of elevation gain, offering scenic views and landmarks.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sacedón offer an active way to explore the picturesque La Alcarria region in Guadalajara, Spain. The area is defined by its expansive reservoirs, Entrepeñas and Buendía, often called the "Sea of Castilla," which are bordered by pine forests, calcareous cliffs, and golden hills. Surrounding mountains, including the Sierra de Caldereros, contribute to a varied terrain suitable for different running preferences. This landscape provides a diverse backdrop for outdoor activities, with routes ranging from gentle paths to more challenging trails. Best jogging routes around Sacedón The most popular jogging route is Route of the Faces, a 7.7 miles (12.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 28 minutes to complete. This unique route features allegorical sculptures carved into sandstone rocks within pine forests. Another top favourite among local runners is Virgen del Socorro Trail, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.3 km) path. This trail offers scenic views over the Tajo River and the Bolarque Reservoir. Local runners also love the Running loop from Alcocer, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the surrounding hills, often completed in about 58 minutes. Jogging around Sacedón is defined by vast reservoirs, pine forests, and rolling hills, with options for both easy, flat runs and more challenging routes with elevation gain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from beginners to experienced runners. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore Sacedón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Sacedón?
A: There are around 9-10 dedicated running routes around Sacedón listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences. These routes cater to different fitness levels, from easy strolls to more challenging runs with significant elevation gain.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the jogging trails in Sacedón?
A: The jogging trails around Sacedón offer a range of difficulties. You'll find 1 easy route perfect for beginners or a relaxed run, 7 moderate routes that provide a good workout, and 1 difficult route for experienced runners seeking a challenge. This variety ensures there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Sacedón?
A: Yes, for families looking for an easier run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Pareja – Dique de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt;. This route is classified as easy, making it suitable for a more relaxed pace with children. The area's natural beauty, especially around the reservoirs, provides a pleasant backdrop for family outings.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails around Sacedón?
A: Many of the natural trails around Sacedón are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept under control and local regulations are followed. The expansive natural landscapes, particularly around the reservoirs and through pine forests, offer plenty of space for you and your canine companion to enjoy. Always ensure your dog is on a leash where required and respect wildlife.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Sacedón?
A: Sacedón is rich in natural beauty and unique landmarks. You can enjoy views of the expansive Entrepeñas and Buendía reservoirs, often called the 'Sea of Castilla'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014429165/route-of-the-faces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt; near Buendía features allegorical sculptures carved into sandstone rocks. Other notable sights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic vistas, and the historic Roman Bridge over the Tagus River.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Sacedón area?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Azud de Pareja – Dique de Pareja loop from Pareja&lt;/a&gt; is an easy circular option. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31507374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; also provides a moderate circular trail, allowing you to start and finish in the same location without retracing your steps.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sacedón?
A: The best times to visit Sacedón for outdoor activities like running are spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more conducive to exploration, offering comfortable temperatures for your runs. Summers can be hot, and winters can be chilly, so plan accordingly if visiting outside of spring or autumn.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails around Sacedón?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, many routes around Sacedón, especially those starting from towns like Alcocer or near popular attractions like the reservoirs, typically have designated or informal parking areas. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking information.

Q: Are there any running routes with significant elevation gain for a challenging workout?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a more challenging run with notable elevation gain, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031416599/virgen-del-socorro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen del Socorro Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route with an elevation gain of approximately 250 meters. For an even more strenuous option, there is one difficult route available in the region, offering a substantial climb for experienced runners.

Q: What makes the running experience in Sacedón unique?
A: The running experience in Sacedón is unique due to its stunning natural setting. You'll run alongside the vast Entrepeñas and Buendía reservoirs, through serene pine forests, and across golden hills. The varied terrain, from gentle paths to routes with significant elevation, combined with the region's historical sites and unique features like the 'Route of the Faces', offers a diverse and memorable backdrop for your runs.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the running trails for scenic breaks?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de los Borrachos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot offering expansive vistas that can enhance any outdoor excursion. Many trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031416599/virgen-del-socorro-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Virgen del Socorro Trail&lt;/a&gt;, also provide scenic views over the Tajo River and the Bolarque Reservoir, perfect for a brief stop.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Sacedón?
A: The running trails around Sacedón are well-regarded by the komoot community. More than 20 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the beautiful reservoir views, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the diversity of routes available for different skill levels. The unique cultural elements, like the sculptures on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014429165/route-of-the-faces" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Faces&lt;/a&gt;, are also frequently highlighted.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Virgen del Socorro Trail?
faqs.answer: This jogging route is approximately 7.3 kilometers long and generally takes about 53 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and any stops you make.

faqs.question: Is the Virgen del Socorro Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate difficulty. While it's suitable for jogging, beginners should be prepared for an elevation gain of nearly 250 meters, which adds a challenge.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for jogging and includes sections of the official Camino del Socorro trail. Expect varied terrain typical for a moderate jogging path, which may include unpaved sections and some inclines due to the elevation gain.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Virgen del Socorro Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter the Monument to the Sacred Heart of Jesus of La Coronilla. You can also enjoy scenic Views of the Entrepeñas Reservoir.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 32% of this jogging route follows sections of the official Camino del Socorro trail.

faqs.question: Is the Virgen del Socorro Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the specific rules for this route aren't detailed, generally, trails in similar natural settings are dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for scenic views, spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging. Summers might be hot, and winters could bring colder conditions, depending on the specific location.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Virgen del Socorro Trail.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Virgen del Socorro Trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the Virgen del Socorro Trail are not provided. However, for routes of this nature, trailheads often have designated parking areas nearby or accessible street parking in the closest town or village.

faqs.question: What should I wear for jogging on this route?
faqs.answer: For a moderate jogging trail with some elevation, wear comfortable athletic clothing suitable for the weather, supportive jogging shoes with good grip, and consider bringing layers. Don't forget water, especially on warmer days.

faqs.question: Are there any advanced jogging routes nearby for experienced runners?
faqs.answer: While this specific route is moderate, the region may offer more challenging trails. Look for routes with higher elevation gains, longer distances, or more technical terrain if you're seeking an advanced experience.
